02 Weekly Company Scorecard Explained - YouTube.mp4
At The Scalable Company, we are big believers in measuring your company's key performance indicators or KPIs on a weekly basis. We like weekly because it's enough time to flatten out all the noise and irregularities that can appear on a daily basis, but not so much time that you're basically flying blind. Here's what I mean. Let's say we need to fly from New York City to Los Angeles, California. Now, what most business owners do is the flight equivalent of taking off in New York. Pointing the plane west, flying six or seven hours, landing, and then wondering why the heck they're so far away from LA. This is what happens when we create an annual plan in general. January and don't look up to see how we're progressing toward that plan until December.
So what's a better way to get from New York to LA? Well, if you know anything about flying, you know that pilots don't necessarily fly in a perfect point-to-point straight line from their starting location to their destination. Things like bad weather, other airplanes, and even instrument malfunction can get them off track. And that's why pilots are continually checking their heading and vectoring, or as we like to call it in the business world, pivoting to keep the plane on course. It's important to note that at any given time, the plane is almost always off course. Again, flying, just like business, is never a straight line. That's why the key to getting to your intended destination is not perfect weather, and it's not a complete lack of distractions and interruptions.
No, the key to getting to your destination is making the necessary pivots in as close to real time as possible. And that's exactly what a weekly scorecard allows you to do. By checking in weekly, you get 12 pivot opportunities, 12 vectoring moments each and every quarter. 52 pivots a year. Now, most entrepreneurs don't check in more than once or twice in an entire year, and yet they wonder why they rarely, if ever, reach their end. intended destination. So our goal for this session is really, really, really simple. We want to get everyone in your company aligned to the same growth targets and provide a platform for communicating progress towards those growth targets across all your different teams. Now, the tool that we're going to leverage to do this is called the company. Scorecard. So, if you haven't yet accessed the company scorecard template, go ahead and get it now because, for the remainder of this session, we're going to be filling it out for your company. Let's get started.
